name: Lothlórien
description: The Golden Wood, held timeless by Galadriel's power. Weather within the bounds of Lórien moves to a different measure than the lands around — the mallorn-leaves do not fall, the air does not bite, and what storms come seem always to have already passed. A traveller leaving Lórien for the Anduin feels the ordinary world as a shock of colder, sharper air.

spring.label: A golden light beneath the mallorn, soft as honey, the air neither warm nor cool.
Silver mist drifting through the trees, lifting slowly toward the canopy.
Fair and still, the forest hushed, no birds but the song of Elves somewhere above.
A gentle rain falling slow — warmer than it has any right to be.
A warm breath of air moves through the wood, bringing the scent of elanor.
Heavy dew on every leaf, the wood lit as if by small silver lamps.
A storm is felt somewhere far off — here only a distant sound and a darker gold.

summer.label: Warm golden light, unhurried — the Wood breathing slowly in the long afternoon.
Fair and soft, the air sweet with flowers that do not grow in mortal lands.
Silver mist at dawn, gone by the time the Elves sing the sun awake.
A warm slow rain, the mallorn-leaves catching most of it, the rest turning silver as it falls.
A stillness so deep the heart seems to slow with it — no breath of wind.
The light deepening toward amber as a distant storm passes beyond the borders.
Rain falling through sunlight, every drop catching the gold — the Wood half-remembered.

autumn.label: The gold of the mallorn unchanged, as it has been since the Wood was made.
Golden mist among the trunks, slow-moving, as if reluctant to leave.
Cool and bright, the air sharper than summer but still kinder than elsewhere.
A quiet rain, cool but not cold, pattering among leaves that will not fall.
A high wind in the canopy, turned by the leaves into something like singing.
A long dusk, the gold turning slowly to dim silver — the Wood between day and night.
A storm moving around the Wood rather than through it — here only a darkened sky.

winter.label: The gold of the mallorn undimmed even in winter — the light simply cooler, thinner.
Clear and cold, the air crystalline, every breath visible in the gold-silver light.
Silver mist carrying frost, every leaf edged white — but the leaves hold.
A still, deep cold that does not cut — the Wood simply asleep in the light.
A brief fall of snow, dusting the paths before a warmer air returns.
A cold quiet rain, washing the silver bark cleaner.
A long slow dusk, the gold fading to blue, the Elven-lamps kindling one by one.
